3581.reason: [The while loop condition uses `token.getLineNo() == lineNumber`, but `token` is updated inside the loop. If the statement spans multiple lines (e.g., a function call with arguments on different lines), the loop will exit prematurely when the line number changes, even if the semicolon hasn't been reached yet. This can cause incorrect parsing of multi-line statements.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition `token.getLineNo() == lineNumber` may cause the loop to terminate prematurely if the semicolon is on a different line than the identifier, leading to incorrect preRead count and tokenListManager state. Additionally, the loop does not handle the case where the token stream ends before finding a semicolon, potentially causing an infinite loop or null pointer exception.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The while loop condition uses `token.getLineNo() == lineNumber`, but `token` is updated inside the loop. If the statement spans multiple lines (e.g., a function call with arguments on different lines), the loop will exit prematurely when the line number changes, even if the semicolon hasn't been reached yet. This can cause incorrect parsing of multi-line statements.]

3594.reason: [This lookback logic is flawed because it assumes all tokens read are on the same line and doesn't handle EOF properly, which can cause incorrect token positioning and missed error detection for missing semicolons or other syntax errors]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The lookBackToken method may not correctly restore the token position if preRead is larger than the actual number of tokens consumed, or if the loop exited due to line number change, causing the parser to be out of sync.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This lookback logic is flawed because it assumes all tokens read are on the same line and doesn't handle EOF properly, which can cause incorrect token positioning and missed error detection for missing semicolons or other syntax errors]

19.reason: [Error: inputStream may be null if file not found; FileLexer constructor will throw NullPointerException]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: inputStream may be null if file not found; FileLexer constructor will throw NullPointerException]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [If the file is not found, inputStream remains null, causing a NullPointerException when passed to FileLexer constructor.]

2181.reason: [Potential NullPointerException: 'next' may be null when the token list is exhausted, causing a crash when calling getTokenType(). A null check (next != null) should be added.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Potential NullPointerException: 'next' may be null when the token list is exhausted, causing a crash when calling getTokenType(). A null check (next != null) should be added.]

2454.reason: [Potential NullPointerException if 'operators' is null. Although initialized in the constructor, adding a null check prevents runtime crashes in edge cases.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Potential NullPointerException if 'operators' is null. Although initialized in the constructor, adding a null check prevents runtime crashes in edge cases.]
